cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why the author wrote the text. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to read the passage again to understand the main idea. The author talks about their experience in an exchange study program in Paris. They mention meeting host parents, moving out when the parents had to leave, and then making a new friend Paolo from Brazil. The author ends by recommending the exchange program to others who want to experience foreign cultures and gain friendships. Looking at the options: A. To show the importance of learning foreign cultures. The author does mention experiencing foreign cultures, but the main focus seems to be on their personal experiences rather than the importance of learning about cultures in general. B. To discuss how to build meaningful friendships. The author does talk about making a friend, Paolo, but the text is more about their personal story than giving advice on building friendships. C. To explain how exchange study programs work. The author gives some details about the program, like the coordinator and moving to a new host family, but the main purpose isn't to explain the structure or process of the program. D. To describe his experiences in an exchange program. This seems to fit best.
